title: Dynamic recrystallization behavior and microstructure evolution of low-density high-strength Fe-Mn-Al-C steel
abstract_summary: The hot deformation behavior of low-density high-strength Fe-Mn-Al-C steel is investigated using Gleeble-3500 compression tests at T = 900–1150 °C and strain rates 0.01–10 s⁻¹. Flow stress curves are analyzed, DRX activation energy (669.844 kJ mol⁻¹) and Zener-Hollomon parameter are determined. Microstructure evolution is characterized, a DRX state diagram is constructed, and a grain size prediction model with high accuracy (R=0.996) is developed to guide hot forging process optimization.
research_objective: To study the dynamic recrystallization behavior and microstructure evolution of low-density high-strength Fe-Mn-Al-C steel during hot deformation in order to establish constitutive models and identify optimal processing windows for grain refinement.
material_systems: nominal composition (wt%): 1.46 C, 25.14 Mn, 10 Al, 0.053 Nb, balance Fe
as-forged billet with initial microstructure (austenite matrix with possible carbides)
main_process_variables: homogenization: heat to 1200 °C at 10 °C/s, hold 120 s, cool to deformation T at 5 °C/s, hold 15 s before compression
expected_information_axis: flow stress characteristics (critical, peak, steady-state stress/strain)
DRX activation energy and Zener-Hollomon parameter
microstructure evolution and DRX grain size
figure evidence (flow curves, optical/confocal micrographs, grain size correlation plots)
key_sections: Introduction: application background of Fe-Mn-Al-C steel, role of DRX in hot working, research motivation and objectives
Materials and methods: material composition, specimen geometry, Gleeble-3500 test matrix, thermal schedule, metallographic preparation (30% nitric acid alcohol etchant), confocal microscopy

Results and discussion: 3.1 analysis of flow stress curves and peak stress sensitivity to T and strain rate; 3.2 calculation of DRX activation energy and Z parameter; 3.3 establishment of characteristic stress/strain models vs. Z, DRX state diagram; 3.4 microstructural evolution with T and strain rate; 3.5 development and validation of average DRX grain size prediction model
Conclusion and prospect: summary of findings, optimal hot working windows (950–1000 °C / 0.01–0.1 s⁻¹ and 1050–1100 °C / 1–10 s⁻¹), suggestion for future multivariate experimental design
